free_text: Measurement of double-differential neutron emission
spectra of 238-U and 232-Th at incident energy 18.0 MeV
Dept. of Nuclear Engineering
main reference,
graphs are given
data obtained via Internet
- experim. details
4.5 MV dynamitron facility, Tohoku Univ
Single-TOF method was used
Pulsed neutron beam less than 1.5 nsec duration
and 2 MHz repetition rate. Deutrons bombarded
tritium loaded metallic Ti target. Post-accelerator
beam chopper was applied in order to improve overall
energy resolution
Liquid NE213 scin detector 14 cm diameter
and 10 cm thickness located about 4 m from sample
detector was coupled to standard time-of-flight
circuit consisting timing discriminator and a
time-to-amplitude converter. Detector system
time-resolution is 2.5 nsec
Another NE213 scintillation detector, 2 inch in diam.
and 2 inch long, was used for neutron monitor to
provide the normalization between sample-in and
sample-out runs.
For energy region lower than
4 MeV
for absolute values of cs
determination
Corrections were made on detectors efficiency and
sample-out background, on finite sample size and back
grounds caused by parasitic and target-scattered,
neutrons, on samples activity
Standard deviation
Data are given by the authors via Internet

and 5 cm height encapsulated in thin aluminium
container and placed 12 cm from the neutron source
